notes: Accepted as valid live evidence: the first bounded live Codex builder run executed end-to-end through the isolated runner on the approved PC. LIVE_RUN_METADATA.json records execution=live, exit_code=0, attempt_count=1, schema_valid=true, scope_valid=true, secret_scan_passed=true, blocked_command_count=0; independently re-verified against schemas/live-run-metadata.schema.json (zero errors) and re-run through check_live_metadata.py (exit 0) from this session.

Integrity independently confirmed, not just re-read: result_sha256 and artifact_sha256s[EDIT_CODEX.md] match a fresh SHA-256 of the committed EDIT_CODEX.md bytes; artifact_sha256s[CHANGES.diff] matches a fresh hash of CHANGES.diff; approval_id_sha256 matches SHA-256('P6-001F-RUN-001'). check_no_secrets.py was re-run independently over all four evidence files (including BLOCKED_COMMANDS.log, which PLAN.md's acceptance criteria and the PC-runner handoff doc omit from the explicit commit list but which P6-001H-EVID's precedent committed) and exits 0 on each. No absolute path, email, or raw session/thread identifier appears in any file; a substring hit on 'session' in LIVE_RUN_METADATA.json is the schema-required authentication_class enum value 'interactive_session', not a leaked token.

EDIT_CODEX.md's front matter independently validates against schemas/edit-summary.schema.json with zero errors: task_id=P6-001F, tool=codex, dry_run=false, files_changed=['fixture.txt'], tests=[]. CHANGES.diff independently passes codex_adapters.validate_synthetic_diff and references only fixture.txt (--- a/fixture.txt / +++ b/fixture.txt, one line added matching the fixture prompt verbatim). check_plan.py exits 0 on PLAN.md. The approval-ledger entry for P6-001F-RUN-001 (vendor codex, role builder, run_date 2026-07-01, rsk_level RSK-1) is present and matches.

This run only succeeded after five real, owner-directed corrections discovered via live PC probes against codex-cli 0.141.0, none of which were guessable from documentation alone: (1) --schema does not exist, replaced with --output-schema <file> requiring a BOM-free file and a schema with uniqueItems/pattern/minLength stripped (OpenAI's structured-output validator rejects them); (2) --budget-usd does not exist and no dollar cost is ever reported, so budget_result is recorded as not_reported (matching the existing Qwen precedent) rather than enforced; (3) the CLI emits a JSONL event stream with file-write metadata only, never a diff, so CHANGES.diff is synthesized by the runner from the actual post-run workspace file rather than extracted from stdout; (4) the ratified model codex-mini-latest is rejected outright with ChatGPT-account auth (HTTP 400) and was re-ratified to gpt-5.4; (5) the ratified 30s timeout was too tight for Codex's multi-round-trip self-verification turns and was re-ratified to 60s after a live run timed out mid-turn.

Two additional defects were found and fixed specifically because a live run was attempted rather than only exercised via fake-CLI contract tests: (a) the process-tree monitor added to close a PATH-shim absolute-path bypass in the same investigation initially killed Codex's own child processes (codex.cmd spawns codex.exe), aborting the run with exit 15 with no diagnostic output, fixed by excluding the running vendor's own name from the monitor's watch set; (b) a Windows WinError 32 on temp-directory cleanup (a lingering Codex background helper briefly holds a workspace file handle) masked an otherwise-successful run, fixed with TemporaryDirectory(ignore_cleanup_errors=True). Neither defect was, or could have been, caught by the existing fake-CLI test suite, since both are specific to real OS process behavior under the real vendor binary.

The git-tolerance policy (owner-ratified 2026-07-01) is a deliberate, recorded risk decision, not a silent weakening of the sandbox: Codex calls git internally for diff-tracking; a live probe confirmed the PATH shim still turns every call into a no-op (git never executes, never touches the network, never writes the workspace — the workspace after the run contains only fixture.txt), so the attempts are recorded transparently (BLOCKED_COMMANDS.log plus the new neutralized_command_count/neutralized_commands metadata fields) instead of failing the run. gh/curl/wget and every other vendor CLI remain fatal on any attempt, including by absolute path. This tolerance and the monitor's self-exclusion fix are the two changes with the most bearing on the sandbox's security properties and deserve independent scrutiny beyond this review.

budget_result=not_reported and the $0.06 ceiling being advisory only (not mechanically enforced by codex-cli 0.141.0) is expected and acceptable, matching the precedent already established and reviewed for the Qwen adapter in P6-001H-EVID.

Approval P6-001F-RUN-001 is single-use and was matched on vendor, role, and run_date; the ledger remains fail-closed and this approval does not authorize a further run, a different model, a higher budget, or a different workspace.

human_review_required=true: this review, the Codex adapter fixes it validates, the monitor self-exclusion fix, and the git-tolerance policy change were all authored within the same model lineage that is also reviewing them here. Independent human sign-off is recommended before treating any of the following as settled: the git-tolerance security tradeoff, the monitor's vendor-self-exclusion logic, and the gpt-5.4/60s re-ratifications, before integrating live Codex into the conductor (P6-001J).
